cover.title: He said 'ik ben heet'
cover.sub: It did not mean what he thought.

panels.speaker: Tom
panels.caption: He means he feels hot. But a Dutch ear hears: I am hot, as in attractive.

panels.speaker: Joost
panels.caption: Joost thinks his friend just announced he's irresistible.

panels.speaker: Tom
panels.caption: He just told the whole terras he's gorgeous.

lessons.title: Don't say 'ik ben heet'
lessons.note: Your body? Always hebben + het. (Cold = ik heb het koud.)

lessons.title: Two words for hot

lines.en: It is hot today. (the weather, and how you feel)

lines.nl: Pas op, de koffie is heet.
lines.en: Careful, the coffee is scorching. (things that can burn you)

lessons.note: Both translate as 'hot'. Use warm for the weather and yourself, heet only for things that can burn you (coffee, a pan, the oven).

cta.title: Save it before the next hot day
cta.sub: Send it to a friend who would say it.
